Assembly: The assembly icCalFusc1.hap1.1 is based on 34x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. X chromosome identified based on BUSCO gene painting using ancestral BUSCO genes. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 5 (1 - 475 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 7 (39,298 - 40,217 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 14 (2,313 - 3,866 & 7,289 - 8,461 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 15 (26,050 - 27,635 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 18 (663 - 2,503 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome Y (1 - 331 & 16,384 - 18,571 Kbp) are unknown.

The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4). The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 2 (4.1 - 5.7 Mbp) and on chromosome 20 (14.8 - 15.2 Mbp) are unknown.

Assembly: The assembly ilIncPrae1.hap2.1 is based on 28x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Assembly: The assembly of Dicentrarchus labrax (fDicLab2) is based on 58X PacBio data and 164X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 6 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 0.477 Mb (with the largest being 0.196 Mb). Additionally, 77 regions totaling 4.917 Mb (with the largest being 0.286 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were namedin order of size.

Assembly: This project provides the alternate haplotype of the diploid genome assembly tzFluFoli1.1 of the broad-leaved hornwrack, Flustra foliacea. The sample was provided by P. Kuklinski and E. Svensen, and the data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). tzFluFoli1.1 is based on 69x PacBio HiFi data and 43x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt, and assembled with hifiasm along with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies (i.e., reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to size.

We did not identify the sex chromosome(s) as sequence data from the heterogametic sex was not available and homology is unreliable for sex chromosome identification in Diptera due to frequent sex chromosome turnover (https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pbio.1002078). The order and orientation of contigs is uncertain on chromosome 2 between 40.19 Mb and 41.9 Mb and on chromosome 4 from 29.9 Mb to the end.

Assembly: The assembly drHedHeli1.2 is based on 29x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using MBG from PacBio HiFi reads mapping to related genomes. A representative circular sequence was selected for each from the graph based on read coverage. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. There is a region of low confidence on Chromosome 1 at 15Mb-26.1Mb consisting of a number of repetitive scaffolds with orientation and order uncertain. HiC data indicates there is a nested heterozygous inversion on Chromosome 3 covering the approximate region 19.7Mb-35.7Mb. On Chromosome 20 there is a heterozygous inversion between approximately 1.6Mb-4.4Mb.

The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. A manually phased assembly (based on HiC signal) was generated from a jointly curated primary and alt assembly. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. HiC data is from different individual, no HiC signal for W-chromosome scaffolds, W chromosome scaffolds are checked with blast to have matches with lepidoptera genomes. Chromosomes Z and W were identified by copy number in the diploid assembly. The order and orientation of scaffolds making up the W chromosomes is uncertain.

Assembly: The assembly of 'Crassostrea rhizophorae' (xbCraRhiz1) is based on 69X PacBio data and 212X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 56 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 1.902 Mb (with the largest being 0.076 Mb). Additionally, 325 regions totaling 47.109 Mb (with the largest being 2.519 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 22 haplotypic regions and 5 contaminant sequences were removed, totaling 10.077Mb and 0.092Mb, respectively (with the largest being 3.054Mb and 0.032Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: Genome assembly of Striarca lactea (xbStrLact1). This assembly is provided by the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly of Striarca lactea (xbStrLact1) is based on 36X PacBio data and 113X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio phased assembly generation with Hifiasm using Hi-C data, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 23 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 1.003 Mb (with the largest being 0.454 Mb). Additionally, 1034 regions totaling 517.205 Mb (with the largest being 8.567 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. A first round of curation was made on the combined map using Pretext followed by a curation made on each haplotype. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size. The most complete chromosomes will be submitted as the primary haplotype.

Assembly: On Oct 10, 2025 this sequence version replaced OU911049.1.; The assembly ilMelMeno1.hap1.4 is based on 50x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The 19 autosomes in Hap1 are named after homology to the previous assembly version. There is a fusion between the W and the previously named autosome 5, the resulting fused chromosome is named W. The non-fused homologous autosome in named Z2. The canonical Z named Z. The 21 autosomes in Hap2 are named based on homology to Hap1. There are 2 heterozygous fission/fusions, the non-fused chromosomes are placed in Hap2 named 1A, 1B, 4A and 4B. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: This project presents the alternate haplotype genome assembly of the Eurasian elk, Alces alces, with a sample provided by Kristian Sather. The data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). The mAlcAlc1.1 assembly is based on 22x PacBio HiFi and 73x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt and assembled with hifiasm together with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies, ensuring that reads for hap1 scaffolding did not contain k-mers unique to hap2, and vice versa. YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes, as confirmed by Hi-C data, are named according to size. Both sex chromosomes have been included in hap1. Contigs with a coverage below 10x were removed from both haplotype assemblies.

Assembly: The assembly of Didemnum vexillum (kaDidVexi2) is based on 104X ONT data and 154X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 164 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 23.101 Mb (with the largest being 8.618 Mb). Additionally, 1611 regions totaling 55.117 Mb (with the largest being 12.259 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 9 haplotypic regions and 22 contaminant sequences were removed, totaling 8.859 Mb and 1.138 Mb, respectively (with the largest being 3.657 Mb and 0.154 Mb). Assembly: The assembly idSuiNota2.1 is based on 52x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. A manually phased assembly (based on HiC signal) was generated from a jointly curated primary and alt assembly. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The PacBio and HiC data are from different samples. The HiC sample comes from a female and the PacBio sample sex is unknown. Based on the single haplotype HiC map, we can see two chromosomes with half coverage, one with HiC signal, which comes to be the X, and other one with no HiC signal, which is a potential Y. Therefore, the Pacbio sample, and consequently the assembly, are from a male. The X chromosome was assigned based on the half coverage on the HiC map and on synteny to Suillia variegata (GCA_949127995.1).

Assembly: The King penguin is an iconic species of the Southern Ocean and is currently being developed as a model-in-the-wild for understanding evolution. We present a chromosome-level assembly of the King penguin (bAptPat1) which is based on 58.5 Gb of Pacbio Revio data and 126 Gbp of Arima Hi-C data. The initial PacBio assembly generation was performed with Hifiasm and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The Nextflow pipeline curationpretext was used to create Hi-C contact maps for manual curation. Manual curation was conducted using PretextView and potential microchromosome scaffolds were identified using MicroFinder. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: On May 17, 2025 this sequence version replaced OZ199644.1.; This project has been funded by the Federation Nationale des Chasseurs (FR). The assembly of Scolopax rusticola (bScoRus3.2) is based on 109X PACBIO data, 35X Ultra-long ONT data and Dovetail Hi-C data. The assembly process included the following steps: initial PACBIO and nanopore (option -ul) assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. No contigs were found to be contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral). Additionally, 96 regions totaling 10.6 Mb (with the largest being 0.89 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size. Sexual chromosomes Z and W were identified using comparative genomics and organization of microchromosomes was curated using MicroFinder.

Assembly: The assembly ilMelMeno1.hap2.4 is based on 50x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The 19 autosomes in Hap1 are named after homology to the previous assembly version. There is a fusion between the W and the previously named autosome 5, the resulting fused chromosome is named W. The non-fused homologous autosome in named Z2. The canonical Z named Z. The 21 autosomes in Hap2 are named based on homology to Hap1. There are 2 heterozygous fission/fusions, the non-fused chromosomes are placed in Hap2 named 1A, 1B, 4A and 4B. Hap1 autosome 2 35.7-37.9 Mb shows high sequence identity to a part of autosome 17 (both haplotypes), likely duplication. This duplication is not present in the homologous Hap2 autosome 2A.

The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. A manually phased assembly (based on HiC signal) was generated from a jointly curated primary and alt assembly. Both haplotypes are assembled to chromosome scale. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosomes in the alt assembly are named after their homolog in the primary assembly. This genome has a complex structure with a number of chromosomes forming multivalent structures. As a result there are a number of different possible formations of the different fused sections of chromosomes. This assembly represents one version. The alternate haplotype (Haplotype 2) represents the second haplotype reflecting the structure of the primary (Haplotype 1). The chromosomes in each haplotype are named in size order and do not reflect each other.

Assembly: The assembly fSpaAur1.2 is based on ~56x PacBio Sequel data, ~62x coverage Illumina HiSeqX data from a 10X Genomics Chromium library generated at the Wellcome Sanger Institute, as well as ~71x coverage HiSeqX data from a Hi-C library prepared by Arima Genomics. An initial PacBio assembly was made using Falcon-unzip, and retained haplotigs were identified using purge_haplotigs. The primary contigs were then scaffolded using the 10X data with scaff10x, then scaffolded further using the Hi-C data with SALSA2. Polishing and gap-filling of both the primary scaffolds and haplotigs was performed using the PacBio reads and Arrow, followed by two rounds of Illumina polishing using the 10X data and freebayes. The mitochondrial assembly was produced at The Rockefeller University using mitoVGP. Finally, the assembly was manually improved using gEVAL to correct mis-joins and improve concordance with the raw data. Chromosomes are named according to synteny with the GCA_003309015.1 assembly of Sparus aurata.

Assembly: 'The assembly of Gracilechinus acutus (eeGraAcut1) is based on 40X PacBio data and Arima HighCoverage Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr).The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 11 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 501 Kb (with the largest being 124 Kb). Additionally, 1,621 regions totaling 1.5 Gb (with the largest being 6.4 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 65 haplotypic regions were removed,totaling 24 Mb (with the largest being 876 Kb)'.

The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome Z was assigned based on coverage, and synteny to Dendrolimus pini (GCA_949752895.1 ). The order and orientation of scaffolds on Chromosome W in the region from ~ 5.13-11.59Mb is unsure.

Assembly: Paternal haplotig of Trio-binned genome of a male wild-type naked mole-rat. Reads in the initial assebmly were a combination of the paternal- and unassigned- reads. Assembly was completed by a combination of the vertebrate genome project and DNA-zoo projects. Mitochondrial genomes were assembled with the mitoVGP pipeline. Y-chromosome contigs were generated with a custom pipeline that levaraged male and female WGS to find strongly male-biased contigs. Male-biased contigs were re-assembled with public male NMR Hi-C data. This assembly was then upraded by employing in silico chromosome painting of the draft scaffolds in the assembly against the American Porcupine (EreDor1) assembly and against the NMR FISH-Karyoptype published in Romanenko et al., 2023 (PMID: 380307020). This upgrade corrected intrachromosomal misassemblies, placed small contigs, and placed centromeres. Ambiguous syntenic alignments were resolved by inspecting our Hi-C-seq data at the end points of those regions. Chromosome names were identified using Karyotype data in collaboration with GeneCodehc.

Assembly: The assembly idNeuQuad1.1 is based on 48x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. A manually phased assembly (based on HiC signal) was generated from a jointly curated primary and alt assembly. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. We did not identify the sex chromosome(s) as sequence data from the heterogametic sex was not available and homology is unreliable for sex chromosome identification in Diptera due to frequent sex chromosome turnover (https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pbio.1002078). Chromosome 4 contains a haplotypic inversion from ~32.5-34 Mbp.

Assembly: The assembly idNemNiti2.1 is based on 113x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. A manually phased assembly (based on HiC signal) was generated from a jointly curated primary and alt assembly. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. We did not identify the sex chromosome(s) as sequence data from the heterogametic sex was not available and homology is unreliable for sex chromosome identification in Diptera due to frequent sex chromosome turnover (https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pbio.1002078).

Assembly: The assembly drUrtDioi1.1 is based on 42x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with HiCanu, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome was curated by taking the assembly of all contigs from canu, performing scaffolding with YAHS and carrying out multiple rounds of curation, initially on all four haplotyps and subsequently on a pseudo-primary haplotype 1.

Assembly: The assembly xcSepEscu2.1 is based on 51x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Aquatic Symbiosis Genomics Project (https://www.aquaticsymbiosisgenomics.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The sex chromosome (Z) was identified by coverage.

Assembly: The assembly xbAnoFrag3.1 is based on 56x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Aquatic Symbiosis Genomics Project (https://www.aquaticsymbiosisgenomics.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The following regions have heterozygous inversions: ~ chromosome 1, 72.9 Mb to 53 Mb; chromosome 2, nested inversion with breakpoints at 81.9 Mb, 116.6 Mb and 140.4 Mb; chromosome 14, 3.5 Mb to 19.5 Mb; chromosome 16, 35.9 Mb to 55.7 Mb.

Taxonomy_x: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Spiralia; Lophotrochozoa; Annelida; Polychaeta; Sedentaria; Canalipalpata; Terebellida; Cirratuliformia; Flabelligeridae; Flabesymbios
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Spiralia; Lophotrochozoa; Annelida; Polychaeta; Sedentaria; Canalipalpata; Terebellida; Cirratuliformia; Flabelligeridae; Flabesymbios
Assembly: The assembly wfFlaComm2.1 is based on 49x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Aquatic Symbiosis Genomics Project (https://www.aquaticsymbiosisgenomics.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: Primary assembly. paternal haplotig of Trio-binned genome of a male wild-type naked mole-rat. Reads in the initial assebmly were a combination of the paternal- and unassigned- reads. Assembly was completed by a combination of the vertebrate genome project and DNA-zoo projects. Mitochondrial genomes were assembled with the mitoVGP pipeline. Y-chromosome contigs were generated with a custom pipeline that levaraged male and female WGS to find strongly male-biased contigs. Male-biased contigs were re-assembled with public male NMR Hi-C data. This assembly was then upraded by employing in silico chromosome painting of the draft scaffolds in the assembly against the American Porcupine (EreDor1) assembly and against the NMR FISH-Karyoptype published in Romanenko et al., 2023 (PMID: 380307020). This upgrade corrected intrachromosomal misassemblies, placed small contigs, and placed centromeres.

Assembly: The assembly sRajBra1.1 is based on 33x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. X chromosome identified based on synteny with Carcharodon carcharias (GCA_017639515.1) and Hypanus sabinus (GCA_030144855.1).

Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The order and orientation of contigs along SUPER_12 between 49 Mb and 56 Mb is uncertain. Assembly: The assembly igLabMino1.1 is based on 24x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MBG from PacBio HiFi reads mapping to related genomes. A representative sequence was selected for each from the graph based on read coverage, contig size, and its alignments to the related genomes, then MitoHiFi was run on this sequence for circularisation and annotation with MitoFinder. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. X chromosome identified based on half coverage relative to autosomes. Y chromosome expected based on this https://doi.org/10.1002/jmor.1050460108 but does not appear to be present in the assembly.

Assembly: The assembly cbCliDend2.1 is based on 38x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. SUPER_4 is likely the U or V sex chromosome. The PacBio (used for de novo assembly) and HiC (used for scaffolding) data are from different samples and the HiC data does not map to SUPER_4 indicating that this chromosome is not present in the HiC sample. Sex is unknown for either sample and so it cannot be determined if SUPER_4 is U or V. SUPER_4 has not been scaffolded due to lack of HiC signal.

Assembly: The assembly drHedHeli1.2 is based on 29x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using MBG from PacBio HiFi reads mapping to related genomes. A representative circular sequence was selected for each from the graph based on read coverage. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. There is a region of low confidence on Chromosome 1 at 15Mb-26.1Mb consisting of a number of repetitive scaffolds with orientation and order uncertain. HiC data indicates there is a nested heterozygous inversion on Chromosome 3 covering the approximate region 19.7Mb-35.7Mb. On Chromosome 20 there is a heterozygous inversion between approximately 1.6Mb-4.4Mb.

The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MBG from the trimmed PacBio HiFi reads. A representative circular sequence was selected and annotated using MITOS WebServer. The produced annotation was used to perform a final MitoHiFi round to allow for the mitocondrial contig rotation and circularisation. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: Paternal haplotig of Trio-binned genome of a male wild-type naked mole-rat. Reads in the initial assebmly were a combination of the paternal- and unassigned- reads. Assembly was completed by a combination of the vertebrate genome project and DNA-zoo projects. Mitochondrial genomes were assembled with the mitoVGP pipeline. Y-chromosome contigs were generated with a custom pipeline that levaraged male and female WGS to find strongly male-biased contigs. Male-biased contigs were re-assembled with public male NMR Hi-C data.

Assembly: Primary assembly. Maternal haplotig of Trio-binned genome of a male wild-type naked mole-rat. Reads in the initial assebmly were a combination of the maternal- and unassigned- reads. Assembly was completed using trio-binning, the Flye aligner, and polishing, 10X-scaffolding, and Hi-C scaffolding piplines established in both the vertebrate genome project and DNA-zoo projects. Mitochondrial genomes were assembled with the mitoVGP pipeline. Y-chromosome contigs were generated with a custom pipeline that levaraged male and female WGS to find strongly male-biased contigs. Male-biased contigs were re-assembled with public male NMR Hi-C data.

Assembly: The assembly dhQueRobu3.1 is based on 37x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. Assembly: The assembly ilPyrMalv3.1 is based on 69x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Qiagen Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. Assembly: The assembly ilCelArgi3.2 is based on 32x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly idNemUlig1.hap1.1 is based on 74x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosomes X and Y were identified by copy number in the diploid assembly, BUSCO gene content and HiC signal. The order and orientation of the scaffolds on the Y chromosome is uncertain.

Assembly: The assembly of Parribacus antarcticus (qmParAntr1) is based on 30X PacBio data and 103X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr).The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 110 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 13.6 Mb (with the largest being 3.2Mb). Additionally, 2,079 regions totaling 272 Mb (with the largest being 1.3 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using oatk. The obtained sequence is linear. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 22 supplementary haplotypic regions, totaling 19.2 Mb (with the largest being 2.3 Mb) were removed. Certain regions showing haplotype profiles have been left out because the long reads coverage has an atypical profile. The genome contains large un-scaffolded and un-placed repetitive regions. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly ilCatFulm1.hap1.1 is based on 83x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z chromosome identified based on BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4). This species presents a Z0 system as no W chromosome was identified. The order and orientation of scaffolds on chromosome 28 between ~14.40-14.84Mb is unsure.

Assembly: Genome assembly of Scorpaena notata. This assembly is provided by the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly of Scorpaena notata (fScoNot1) is based on 82X PacBio data and 220X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 2 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 0.102 Mb (with the largest being 0.076 Mb). Additionally, 160 regions totaling 9.826 Mb (with the largest being 2.099 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. Mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. During manual curation, 1 haplotypic region was removed, totaling 5.5Mb. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly ilIdaRust2.1 is based on 76x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. A manually phased assembly (based on HiC signal) was generated from a jointly curated primary and alt assembly. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The sex chromosomes were assigned based on the copy number on the diploid HiC map. The PacBio sample is from a female sample and the HiC comes from a male sample. Although, we could identify the W chromosome, it has no HiC signal, what didn't enable us to assemble it and infer about the order and orientation of the scaffolds within it. The first scaffold of W chromosome has a telomere signal on the start and has a small region of similarity with the Z chromosome, which was used to orientate the Z and W chromosomes.

Assembly: The assembly ihSopOrie1.hap1.1 is based on 56x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated as part of the European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/) via the Biodiversity Genomics Europe project (BGE, https://biodiversitygenomics.eu/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. X chromosomes were assigned based on synteny to GCA_964468525.

Assembly: The assembly of Atrina seminuda (xbAtrSemi1) is based on 90X PacBio data and 211X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 11 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 3.616 Mb (with the largest being 3.115 Mb). Additionally, 139 regions totaling 57.041 Mb (with the largest being 4.864 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. During manual curation, 17 haplotypic regions were removed, totaling 41Mb (with the largest being 1.4Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. Assembly: The assembly ihSteHols2.1 is based on 39x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. A manually phased assembly (based on HiC signal) was generated from a jointly curated primary and alt assembly. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The X chromosome was identified based on synteny with Leptopterna dolabrata.
